macro_segments.title: Holacha She-lo Baragel: Does Non-Foot Conveyance Count as Holacha?

The Question of Efshar Letikuna: Can a Failed Non-Foot Conveyance Be Fixed?
Mishna Evidence: Kibel Hachasher V'Natan L'Pasul—Reversibility Suggested
Gemara's Clarification: Direction of Movement Matters—Exterior vs. Interior

macro_segments.title: Spilled Blood During Holacha: Can It Be Collected and Used Despite Non-Foot Movement?

micro_segments.title: The Case of Spilled Blood: Nishpach Min Hakli Al Haritzpa V'Asfo Kasher
The Problem: Blood Moving Toward Mizbeach Through Spillage Without Walking
Gemara's Alternatives: Incline, Indentation, or Coagulation Prevent Forward Movement
Proof from Another Perek: Nishpach Al Hartzpa Without Initial Kabbalah Is Invalid
Conclusion: Even Movement Toward Mizbeach Outside of Walking Does Not Invalidate

macro_segments.title: Reinterpreting Rabbi Shimon and the Rabbanan: Holacha Zutrata vs. Holacha Rabta

micro_segments.title: Gemara's Radical Proposal: The Debate Is About Holacha She-lo Baragel, Not Normal Conveyance
Distinction Between Holacha Rabta (Large) and Holacha Zutrata (Small)
Application to Chatas Ha'of: Where Invalidating Intent Falls
Rabbi Yirmeyah's Question: When the Kohen's Hand Is Cut Mid-Sprinkle
Resolution: The Entire Avodah Extends Until the Blood Hits the Mizbeach
Reversal: Rav Pappa and Rav Huna Reject the Reinterpretation from Eretz Yisrael
Correction: Rabbi Shimon Debated on Holacha Rabta, Not on Holacha Zutrata

macro_segments.title: Holacha by a Zar: Can a Non-Kohen's Conveyance Be Corrected by a Kohen?

micro_segments.title: The Problem: A Zar Performs Holacha from Point A to Point X
The Debate: Efshar Letikuna vs. Lo Efshar Letikuna—Can a Kohen Fix It?
Reverse Scenario: Kohen Starts, Then Zar Reverses and Reconveys
Rav Shimi Bar Ashi's Analysis: The Debate Flips Based on Who Acts Last
Rava's Correction: Tsarich L'Amtuye—Ongoing Obligation Makes Both Cases Invalid

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's View: Necessary vs. Unnecessary Movement in Holacha

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer's Position: Holacha Becomes Invalidating Only When Movement Is Necessary
Rava's Reframing: The Debate Centers on Doubling Back, Not Direction Alone
Everyone Agrees: Movement Toward Mizbeach Is Necessary; Away Is Not
The Real Debate: If You Move Away Then Back, Is the Return Movement 'Necessary'?
Abaye's Challenge: The Braita Clarifies That Even Eliezer Sees Return as Necessary
Resolution: Rabbi Eliezer Simply Disagrees with His Father on 'In Theory' vs. 'In Practice'

macro_segments.title: Shift in Focus: Kol HaZvachim Shekiblu Daman—Who Invalidates Through Person, Not Intent

micro_segments.title: New Perek: Introducing Categories of People Who Invalidate Sacrifices
The Mishna's List: Zar, Onen, Shikor, Ba'al Mum, T'vul Yom, Mechussar Kipurim, and Others
Gemara's Method: Deriving Zar's Invalidation from Pessukim and Kal V'Chomer
Kal V'Chomer from Ba'al Mum: If One with a Blemish Invalidates, So Should a Zar
Common Denominator: Anyone Warned Against Performing Avodah Invalidates If He Does
